raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to find and delete .txt files recursively in /system, answering y to any prompt. Use find with -delete? But to answer y to prompts, maybe use rm -i? But we want to auto-yes. Use rm -f. So command: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -f {} +. That's it.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command to find and delete .txt files recursively in /system, answering y to any prompt. Use find with -delete? But to answer y to prompts, maybe use rm -i? But we want to auto-yes. Use rm -f. So command: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -f {} +. That's it.
